excerpts.text: I am not the betrayer of the Lord Jesus Christ. I am not Judas. I have been Peter. I denied my Lord, but I have repented.
excerpts.source_title: Statement after recanting his forced confession

excerpts.text: We — because of the China Inland Mission, not the Lord — we are the people of God. Where does Peter's denial end, and the truth begin?
The faith which was once delivered to the saints — this is what I preach. Not a new gospel, not a social gospel, but the old, old gospel of Jesus Christ and Him crucified.
The modernists deny the virgin birth, the bodily resurrection, the miracles, and the atonement. Can we join hands with those who deny the very foundations of our faith?
They asked me to join the Three-Self Patriotic Movement. I told them: I cannot join a body that is under the control of unbelievers. My church is under Christ alone.
A church that is controlled by the state is no longer a church of Christ. It is a political organization wearing a religious mask.
I was born during the Boxer Rebellion. My mother hid in the legation quarters. Before I drew my first breath, I was already in the furnace of suffering for Christ.
excerpts.source_title: Autobiography, Fifty Years of My Christian Life

excerpts.text: I would rather go to prison than betray my Lord. A man who is faithful only when it costs him nothing is not faithful at all.
In the twenty-three years of my imprisonment, I have learned what Paul meant when he said, 'I have learned in whatsoever state I am therewith to be content.'
If your sins are not cleansed by the blood of Christ, no amount of good works can make you acceptable before God.
The greatest danger to the church is not persecution from without, but compromise from within.
I am called 'Mingdao' — 'Testify to the Way.' This name was given to me by God through my mother's prayers. It is my calling, and I dare not abandon it.
excerpts.source_title: Fifty Years of My Christian Life

excerpts.text: I was a young teacher in a Christian school when I discovered that my principal did not believe in the resurrection of Christ. From that day, I understood the battle.
excerpts.source_title: Fifty Years of My Christian Life

excerpts.text: A servant of God must not be a man-pleaser. He must speak the truth whether men like it or not.
I wrote my confession under pressure. I am now revoking every word of it. I would rather die than live as a liar.
Christ said: Render unto Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and unto God the things that are God's. But my conscience belongs to God, not to Caesar.
During the Japanese occupation, I refused to register my church with the puppet government. Now I refuse to register it with this government. The principle is the same.
A preacher must have a clean heart and a clean life. If his life contradicts his message, he is worse than an unbeliever.
I have been beaten, I have been humiliated, I have been imprisoned. But they cannot touch my soul. That belongs to Christ.
Do not ask the government to protect the church. Ask God. A church that relies on political power has already lost its spiritual power.
My wife and I were arrested together. They separated us for twenty years. But God kept us both. Our faith did not fail.
The Bible is the Word of God — every word of it. I stake my life on it. I have staked my life on it.
Real Christians are few. Many who call themselves Christians are merely following a tradition, attending a ritual, or seeking social advantage. They do not know the living Christ.
When I was released from prison, I was nearly blind. But I could see more clearly than ever before. Suffering refines the vision of the soul.
I spent my youth preaching the gospel in the streets of Beijing. The authorities warned me. The modernists mocked me. But God honored His Word.
excerpts.source_title: Fifty Years of My Christian Life
